title: Create a physical location tied to a jurisdiction
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to create a location that represents a physical office or operational site belonging to a single jurisdiction, so that the company's places of operation are accurately recorded against the correct legal jurisdiction.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am creating a new location, when I save it, then it represents a physical office or operational site.; Given I am creating a new location, when I save it, then it must be associated with exactly one jurisdiction (belonging to a single jurisdiction).; Given a location has been created, when I view its details, then it is presented as a physical office or operational site with its single jurisdiction shown.; Given I attempt to save a location without a jurisdiction, when I submit, then the system prevents the save and prompts me to select exactly one jurisdiction.; Given I attempt to assign more than one jurisdiction to a single location, when I submit, then the system rejects it because a location belongs to only one jurisdiction.

title: Scope locations to a specific company by default
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want locations to be company-specific by default and not shared across related companies unless explicitly configured, so that a company's operational sites are not unintentionally exposed to or used by other related companies.
acceptance_criteria: Given I create a location, when it is saved, then it is owned by and scoped to my company by default.; Given a location is company-specific, when a user from another related company browses locations, then that location is not visible or selectable to them.; Given related companies exist, when no group-company sharing has been explicitly configured, then locations are not shared across those related companies by default.; Given a location is company-specific, when it is used within my company, then only my company can reference it unless explicit sharing is configured.

title: Maintain multiple locations within the same jurisdiction
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to create and maintain multiple locations within the same jurisdiction, so that a company operating several sites in one jurisdiction can represent each of them.
acceptance_criteria: Given a jurisdiction already has one or more locations for my company, when I add another location in that jurisdiction, then the system allows it without conflict or uniqueness error.; Given multiple locations exist in the same jurisdiction, when I view the company's locations, then all of them are listed for that jurisdiction.; Given I add, edit, or remove any of several locations in the same jurisdiction, when I save, then each location is maintained independently.

title: Share a location owned by one company with other group companies
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to configure a location owned by one company to be referenced by other companies within the same group-company structure, so that group companies can reuse a shared physical site instead of duplicating it.
acceptance_criteria: Given a location owned by one company, when I configure sharing within the group-company structure, then other companies in the same group can reference that location.; Given a location is shared, when a referencing company uses it, then ownership remains with the originating (owning) company and referencing companies only hold a reference.; Given companies are not part of the same group-company structure, when I attempt to share a location between them, then the system does not allow the reference.; Given a shared location, when the owning company modifies or removes it, then referencing companies see the change consistent with the single owned source.

title: Require explicit configuration to share a location
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want location sharing across group companies to require explicit configuration, so that no location is shared implicitly or by accident.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company-specific location, when no sharing has been explicitly configured, then it remains scoped to its owning company only.; Given I want another group company to reference a location, when I explicitly enable sharing, then the reference becomes available only after that configuration is confirmed.; Given sharing has not been explicitly configured, when any group company attempts to reference the location, then the reference is not available.; Given I disable a previously configured sharing, when I save, then referencing companies can no longer reference that location.

title: Maintain an audit trail for shared locations
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want every location-sharing configuration and change to be tracked with an audit trail, so that changes to cross-company location access are traceable and accountable.
acceptance_criteria: Given I configure, change, or revoke location sharing across group companies, when the change is saved, then an audit trail entry is recorded.; Given a shared location has an audit trail, when I review it, then I can see the sharing configuration changes that were made, including what changed and that they were performed.; Given multiple sharing changes occur over time, when I review the audit trail, then each change is recorded as a distinct traceable entry.

title: Prevent sharing of locations outside a group-company relationship
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the platform to enforce that locations are never shared across related companies unless a group-company relationship is explicitly configured, so that data isolation between unrelated companies is preserved.
acceptance_criteria: Given two companies without a configured group-company relationship, when either attempts to reference the other's location, then the system blocks the reference.; Given related companies exist but no group-company sharing is configured, when either browses the other's locations, then those locations are not shared or visible.; Given a group-company relationship exists, when sharing is explicitly configured, then the system permits referencing only within that group structure.; Given a group-company relationship is removed, when a company still references a shared location, then the system enforces that the cross-company reference is no longer permitted.

title: View and manage the company's location list
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to view and manage all locations belonging to my company, so that I have an accurate, up-to-date record of the company's physical places of operation.
acceptance_criteria: Given my company owns one or more locations, when I open the locations list, then all locations owned by my company are shown with their jurisdiction.; Given a location is shared to my company from another group company, when I view the list, then it is clearly distinguished as referenced (not owned) by my company.; Given I select a location I own, when I edit or update its details, then the changes are saved and scoped to my company.; Given I attempt to edit a location my company only references (does not own), then the system prevents ownership-level changes because ownership remains with the originating company.

title: Reference a shared group-company location within my company
user_story: As a Company Admin of a referencing company, I want to use a location that another company in my group-company structure has shared, so that I can operate from a shared physical site without duplicating the location record.
acceptance_criteria: Given another company in my group has explicitly shared a location, when I browse selectable locations, then the shared location is available for me to reference.; Given I reference a shared location, when it is used within my company, then ownership of the location remains with the originating company.; Given a location has not been explicitly shared with my company, when I browse locations, then it is not available to reference.; Given sharing of a location to my company is revoked, when I next access it, then the reference is no longer available.

title: View the location assigned to me
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to view the location I am associated with, so that I know which physical office or operational site and jurisdiction I belong to.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am associated with a location, when I view my profile or location details, then the location is shown as a physical office or operational site with its jurisdiction.; Given my location is company-specific, when I view it, then I only see the location relevant to my company (or shared to it), and not unrelated companies' locations.; Given I am an employee, when I view a location, then I cannot modify its configuration, sharing, or ownership.

title: Persist locations under tenant-scoped storage with row-level isolation
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want every location record to be persisted in tenant-scoped storage with row-level security keyed to its owning company, so that the company-specific default is enforced at the data layer and cannot be bypassed by the application or API.
acceptance_criteria: Given a location is created, when it is written to storage, then it carries the owning company's tenant scope and is protected by row-level security.; Given a query is issued for locations, when the requesting principal belongs to a company, then only that company's owned locations plus locations explicitly shared to it via a group-company relationship are returned by the data layer.; Given a request from a company with no ownership or configured share, when it queries another company's location by id, then the data layer returns no row regardless of application-layer logic.; Given tenant isolation is in force, when any location read or write occurs, then it is constrained to the tenant scope of the owning company.

title: Maintain referential integrity between shared references and their owned location
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want a shared location to be modeled as a single owned record referenced by other companies, with referential integrity and effective-dated history maintained, so that shared references always resolve to a valid owned source and every change is historically reconstructable.
acceptance_criteria: Given a location is shared, when a referencing company holds it, then the data model stores one owned record and separate reference links, not duplicated location records.; Given a reference link exists, when the owned location is present, then the reference resolves to exactly that owned record via a foreign-key relationship.; Given the owning company modifies the location, when the change is committed, then referencing companies resolve to the same updated owned record with no divergence.; Given a location or its sharing changes over time, when I inspect its history, then the effective-dated record shows what the value was at any point in time.; Given an attempt to reference a non-existent or unshared owned location, when the reference is written, then integrity constraints reject it.

title: Manage location sharing as governed, versioned configuration
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want location-sharing relationships to be stored as governed, versioned, effective-dated configuration that the platform reads at runtime, so that who-shares-what-with-whom can be changed without code and every version is retained.
acceptance_criteria: Given I enable, change, or revoke a sharing relationship, when I save, then a new configuration version is created and effective-dated rather than overwriting the prior state.; Given the platform evaluates whether a company may reference a location, when it resolves access, then it reads the current governed sharing configuration as of the effective date.; Given a sharing configuration is changed, when I review its history, then prior versions remain retrievable and each version is tied to its audit-trail entry.; Given a sharing change is scheduled with a future effective date, when that date is reached, then the reference availability updates automatically without a code change.

title: Search and filter the locations grid by jurisdiction and ownership
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a metadata-driven locations grid where I can search and filter locations by jurisdiction and by owned-versus-referenced status, so that I can quickly find and manage the right site among many.
acceptance_criteria: Given my company has many locations across jurisdictions, when I open the locations grid, then I can filter the list by jurisdiction and see only matching locations.; Given owned and referenced (shared-in) locations both appear, when I filter by ownership status, then I can view only owned, only referenced, or all locations.; Given I search by name or attribute, when I type a query, then the grid returns matching locations with their jurisdiction and ownership indicator.; Given the grid is metadata-driven, when displayed columns or filters are configured, then the presentation reflects that configuration without code changes.
